USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (no sugar added) is the closest approved-source match. All core macros are confirmed zero or trace by formulation for an unsweetened brewed tea beverage.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea contains negligible carbohydrates (0.2 g/100 g, 0.6 g per 300 ml serving). Glycemic index is not applicable as the carbohydrate content is below the threshold required to elicit a meaningful glycaemic response. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey the true clinical interpretation. GL per serving is effectively 0.0. No GI testing is required or available for this product category.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (8 mg/100 g; 24 mg per 300 ml bottle) and zero added sugar; green tea catechins have evidence-based vasodilatory effects.
hypertension.guidance: Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension; sodium content is negligible at 24 mg per bottle, and regular consumption of green tea catechins (EGCG) is associated with modest reductions in systolic and diastolic blood pressure in meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials — however, caffeine-sensitive individuals should limit intake to 2–3 bottles per day to avoid acute blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar, negligible carbohydrates (0.2 g/100 g), and near-zero calories; EGCG catechins have evidence-based insulin-sensitising properties.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it contributes no meaningful glycaemic load (GL ≈ 0 per serving), and the polyphenol EGCG has been shown in clinical studies to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce postprandial glucose — confirm the product label shows no added sweeteners before consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, negligible sodium, and high polyphenol content (EGCG) with evidence-based cardioprotective effects.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea is suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease; it contains no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium, while green tea catechins have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation, improved endothelial function, and lower cardiovascular event risk in prospective cohort studies — moderate daily consumption of 2–4 cups is supported by current evidence.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EGCG polyphenols directly inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6) relevant to RA pathophysiology.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; EGCG is among the most studied anti-inflammatory polyphenols for RA, and regular consumption may complement pharmacological management — avoid consuming within 1 hour of iron-rich meals as tannins may inhibit non-haem iron absorption, which is particularly relevant for RA patients on iron supplementation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea (綾鷹 緑茶 無糖) is a Japanese bottled green tea beverage produced by Coca-Cola Japan under the Ayataka brand, brewed from whole Camellia sinensis tea leaves using a traditional Japanese brewing method that preserves the natural umami and astringency of the leaf. The beverage contains no added sugar, no artificial sweeteners, and no flavourings, making it a pure, minimally processed green tea drink. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 1 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0.2 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, and 8 mg sodium — a nutritional profile consistent with brewed unsweetened green tea. The flavour profile is characterised by mild vegetal and grassy notes with a light astringency from tannins and a subtle umami depth from L-theanine and amino acids naturally present in the tea leaf. Clinically, Ayataka Unsweetened Green Tea is notable for its high content of catechin polyphenols, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which has been extensively studied for its insulin-sensitising, vasodilatory, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective properties. It is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-suitable. A standard serving is one 300 ml bottle, providing approximately 3 kcal and 24 mg sodium — negligible contributions to daily intake. It is widely available in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets and is commonly consumed as a daily hydration beverage, a meal accompaniment, or a health-conscious alternative to sugary drinks.

Does Ayataka green tea have sugar?
How many calories in Ayataka green tea?

What are the health benefits of Ayataka green tea?
Does Ayataka green tea contain caffeine?

notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is naturally zero — unsweetened brewed green tea contains no protein by formulation and is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. fat_g is naturally zero — unsweetened brewed green tea contains no fat by formulation and is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. carbohydrate_g is set to 0.2 g/100 g (trace) consistent with USDA data for brewed green tea; the HPB input reported 0.0 g which is within rounding tolerance. energy_kcal is set to 1.0 kcal/100 g consistent with USDA data; HPB input reported 0.0 kcal which is within rounding tolerance for a near-zero calorie beverage. Glycemic index note: glycemic_index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value to satisfy the requirement that the field be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey the correct clinical interpretation — this beverage contains negligible carbohydrates (0.2 g/100 g, 0.6 g per 300 ml serving) and does not elicit a meaningful glycaemic response. Negative sentinel values: vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed micronutrient data for bottled Ayataka green tea specifically are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026; brewed green tea entries in USDA do not report these micronutrients at meaningful levels. Sodium value of 8 mg/100 g preserved from HPB input record, consistent with USDA range. Portion size of 300 g (1 bottle) preserved from input. The input per_100g_meta listed cholesterol, saturated_fat, and trans_fat sourced from grabfood — these have been superseded by USDA FoodData Central values (all confirmed zero for brewed unsweetened green tea). Grabfood is a forbidden source per Project Veritas v2 source policy and has not been used as primary evidence in this output.
